title: Dumping Channels (WADs)
requirements: An SD card or USB drive, FAT32 formatted
_note: Dumps an installed channel (game, Wii Menu, IOS, etc.) as a WAD file using Yet Another BlueDump MOD — useful for backing up channels before removing them, or for use with shortcut/forwarder tools.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body: **Yet Another BlueDump MOD** reads content already installed on your Wii's system memory and saves it as a `.wad` file. First, let's check your SD card or USB drive is FAT32.

steps.title: Put BlueDump MOD on your SD card
steps.body: Graphite downloads **Yet Another BlueDump MOD** and unpacks it into the `apps` folder on your SD card, ready to launch from the Homebrew Channel.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Move the SD card to your Wii
steps.body: Take the SD card out of your computer and slot it into your Wii (or plug in the USB drive). Power on the console and press **I did this**.

steps.title: Dump the channel
steps.body:
1. Open the **Homebrew Channel** and launch **Yet Another BlueDump MOD**.
2. Press **A**.
3. Choose **Installed Channel Titles**.
4. Find the content you want to back up and press **1**.
5. Select **Backup to WAD**.
6. When asked about fakesigning the **ticket**, answer **Yes**.
7. When asked about fakesigning the **TMD**, answer **No**.
8. When asked about changing the output WAD's region, answer **No**.

The WAD file saves to a folder on your storage device.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: Your dumped WAD is saved on the SD card/USB drive. You can reinstall it later with a WAD manager like YAWM ModMii Edition, or use it with shortcut tools like WiiGSC.
